long_description: Fabric SRE received report from Compute QA that they are not seeing any jira branch in staging0901. Only stable branch is visible
short_description: Retrospective INC - IMS Release Branch deleted by a scheduled Jenkins Cron Job
close_notes: Scheduled Jenkins Cron Job ran which deleted the release branch. To fix the issue, the release branch was redeployed
chronology:
Timelines are in UTC 

04.12.2025 

07:06 UTC - Compute QA team reported the issue that the release branch got deleted somehow and only the stable branch was found. The issue was escalated to DevAuto.
07:16 UTC - FSRE created and escalated the jira for tracking to DevAuto. FABREQ-75912 User not seeing any jira branch in staging0901
07:32 UTC - DevAuto found that the Weekly_Delete_Staging_Branches has run which has deleted the Release branch from the staging server and after that the Deploy Stable Staging job has run as a downstream job thus only the stable branch is there and not the release branch.
07:34 UTC - Compute QA asked to deploy the latest release branch as they were doing BM regression. FSRE asked them to update the same in the warroom.
07:50 UTC - FSRE enquired from compute QA that VSI regression has completed testing so will it need to start again. Compute QA informed that only a sanity check would be enough, only BM testing needs to be performed.
08:14 UTC - The teams call was initiated and the stakeholders to join it.
12:10 UTC - Cheryll enquired Compute QA team about the time that it will take for BM regression to complete testing. Compute QA gave an approximation of 8-10 hours.
12:24 UTC - Cheryll completed rebuilding the release build from scratch and Compute QA triggered BM provisioning.
17:24 UTC - Compute QA notified that the BM regression is still in progress
17:48 UTC - Dev Release Managers were notified that the IMS Release JIRA-4819 is cancelled
